options_considered: Human-only orchestration
Antigravity lead orchestration
Peer-to-peer self-coordination
decisions.rationale: Single lead minimizes ambiguity and merge-order confusion across concurrent agents.

options_considered: Feature branches per epic
Trunk with short branches
Patch queue without branches
decisions.rationale: Continuous small merges are the strongest conflict reducer for this pace.

options_considered: No ownership
Soft ownership
Strict folder ownership
decisions.rationale: Explicit lane boundaries reduce overlap and integration risk.

options_considered: Single checkout with branches only
Worktree optional
Mandatory worktree per task
decisions.rationale: Per-task filesystem isolation reduces branch and dirty-tree collisions across concurrent agents.

decisions.topic: Scope expansion for T-20260211-001 Expo bootstrap files
options_considered: Keep narrow scope and create non-runnable shell fragments
Expand scope to include minimal Expo root config files
decisions.selected_option: Expand scope to include minimal Expo root config files
decisions.rationale: A runnable Expo Router shell requires root config files outside app/(tabs).

decisions.topic: Cross-lane hotfix to analytics provider parse/type signature
options_considered: Leave provider parse issue and ship onboarding analytics without type checks
Apply minimal provider hotfix while implementing T-20260211-003
decisions.selected_option: Apply minimal provider hotfix while implementing T-20260211-003
decisions.rationale: Unblocked onboarding analytics imports by fixing a malformed doc comment and navigationRef addListener type in provider.tsx without changing analytics runtime behavior.

options_considered: Document-only typography guidance
Hard policy plus automated validator
decisions.rationale: Prevents recurring font misuse by making Zalando default UI mandatory and enforcing restricted use of Lora/Playfair/Amiri via script.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Journey streak redesign
options_considered: Wait for a claude-lane implementer to take Journey changes
Allow codex to execute the Journey rework in an isolated task worktree
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute the Journey rework in an isolated task worktree
decisions.rationale: The user requested immediate implementation and the work can remain fully isolated to src/features/journey/** without expanding into shared navigation or settings files.

decisions.topic: Allow larger diff for T-20260307-022 Journey state and UI rewrite
options_considered: Split Journey rework into separate storage and UI tasks
Allow one larger isolated task because the old routine-first model had to be replaced coherently
decisions.selected_option: Allow one larger isolated task because the old routine-first model had to be replaced coherently
decisions.rationale: The user requested a full Journey/Streaks restructuring, and splitting the storage migration from the UI rewrite would have prolonged an incoherent intermediate state.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Tools and Journey UI title polish
options_considered: Leave the task to the default owning lanes and wait for reassignment
Allow codex to execute a narrow cross-surface polish pass after explicit human approval
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a narrow cross-surface polish pass after explicit human approval
decisions.rationale: The requested change is a small, tightly scoped UI pass across the tools tab screen and journey feature components, and the human explicitly authorized reassignment to keep momentum.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Home hadith card polish
options_considered: Wait for an opencode-lane implementer to adjust the Home quote surface
Allow codex to execute a one-file Home hadith polish pass
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a one-file Home hadith polish pass
decisions.rationale: The requested change is tightly scoped to a single home feature component and can be completed safely without widening the diff or blocking on lane reassignment.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Tools and Journey card rebalance
options_considered: Split the redesign by ownership lane and wait for separate implementers
Allow codex to execute one cohesive redesign pass across the Tools hero and Journey hub surfaces
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute one cohesive redesign pass across the Tools hero and Journey hub surfaces
decisions.rationale: The requested redesign is intentionally about shared visual language between the Tools and Journey cards, so splitting it across lanes would increase iteration risk and produce a less coherent result.

decisions.topic: Product direction reset to imaan.app native intervention product
options_considered: Continue extending the Path of Nur PWA roadmap
Re-baseline the repo around the imaan.app PRD and native-first architecture
decisions.selected_option: Re-baseline the repo around the imaan.app PRD and native-first architecture
decisions.rationale: The product primitive, app IA, and backend needs have changed materially. Continuing to extend the old roadmap would create compounding architectural debt.

options_considered: Firebase-centered stack
Supabase with Azure-hosted app server
VM-only custom backend
decisions.rationale: Supabase is the system of record for auth, relational data, storage, and vectors, while Azure Container Apps hosts the orchestration API and workers using existing credits. OpenRouter is server-side only.

decisions.topic: Cross-boundary exception for repo re-baseline and backend foundation
options_considered: Split the repo reset across multiple owners before any implementation
Allow codex to execute one coordinated reset task across AGENTS, collab state, shared contracts, and backend scaffold
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute one coordinated reset task across AGENTS, collab state, shared contracts, and backend scaffold
decisions.rationale: The repo instructions, task queue, and code architecture all encode the old product. A single coordinated reset is the fastest way to stop new work from landing on stale assumptions.
